root_text.an3.109:1.1: Atha kho anāthapiṇḍiko gahapati yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho anāthapiṇḍikaṁ gahapatiṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an3.109:1.2: “citte, gahapati, arakkhite kāyakammampi arakkhitaṁ hoti, vacīkammampi arakkhitaṁ hoti, manokammampi arakkhitaṁ hoti.
root_text.an3.109:1.3: Tassa arakkhitakāyakammantassa arakkhitavacīkammantassa arakkhitamanokammantassa kāyakammampi avassutaṁ hoti, vacīkammampi avassutaṁ hoti, manokammampi avassutaṁ hoti.
root_text.an3.109:1.4: Tassa avassutakāyakammantassa avassutavacīkammantassa avassutamanokammantassa kāyakammampi pūtikaṁ hoti, vacīkammampi pūtikaṁ hoti, manokammampi pūtikaṁ hoti.
root_text.an3.109:1.5: Tassa pūtikāyakammantassa pūtivacīkammantassa pūtimanokammantassa na bhaddakaṁ maraṇaṁ hoti, na bhaddikā kālakiriyā.
root_text.an3.109:2.1: Seyyathāpi, gahapati, kūṭāgāre ducchanne kūṭampi arakkhitaṁ hoti, gopānasiyopi arakkhitā honti, bhittipi arakkhitā hoti;
root_text.an3.109:2.2: kūṭampi avassutaṁ hoti, gopānasiyopi avassutā honti, bhittipi avassutā hoti;
root_text.an3.109:2.3: kūṭampi pūtikaṁ hoti, gopānasiyopi pūtikā honti, bhittipi pūtikā hoti.
root_text.an3.109:3.1: Evamevaṁ kho, gahapati, citte arakkhite kāyakammampi arakkhitaṁ hoti, vacīkammampi arakkhitaṁ hoti, manokammampi arakkhitaṁ hoti.
root_text.an3.109:3.2: Tassa arakkhitakāyakammantassa arakkhitavacīkammantassa arakkhitamanokammantassa kāyakammampi avassutaṁ hoti, vacīkammampi avassutaṁ hoti, manokammampi avassutaṁ hoti.
root_text.an3.109:3.3: Tassa avassutakāyakammantassa avassutavacīkammantassa avassutamanokammantassa kāyakammampi pūtikaṁ hoti, vacīkammampi pūtikaṁ hoti, manokammampi pūtikaṁ hoti.
root_text.an3.109:3.4: Tassa pūtikāyakammantassa pūtivacīkammantassa pūtimanokammantassa na bhaddakaṁ maraṇaṁ hoti, na bhaddikā kālakiriyā.
root_text.an3.109:4.1: Citte, gahapati, rakkhite kāyakammampi rakkhitaṁ hoti, vacīkammampi rakkhitaṁ hoti, manokammampi rakkhitaṁ hoti.
root_text.an3.109:4.2: Tassa rakkhitakāyakammantassa rakkhitavacīkammantassa rakkhitamanokammantassa kāyakammampi anavassutaṁ hoti, vacīkammampi anavassutaṁ hoti, manokammampi anavassutaṁ hoti.
root_text.an3.109:4.3: Tassa anavassutakāyakammantassa anavassutavacīkammantassa anavassutamanokammantassa kāyakammampi apūtikaṁ hoti, vacīkammampi apūtikaṁ hoti, manokammampi apūtikaṁ hoti.
root_text.an3.109:4.4: Tassa apūtikāyakammantassa apūtivacīkammantassa apūtimanokammantassa bhaddakaṁ maraṇaṁ hoti, bhaddikā kālakiriyā.
root_text.an3.109:5.1: Seyyathāpi, gahapati, kūṭāgāre succhanne kūṭampi rakkhitaṁ hoti, gopānasiyopi rakkhitā honti, bhittipi rakkhitā hoti;
root_text.an3.109:5.2: kūṭampi anavassutaṁ hoti, gopānasiyopi anavassutā honti, bhittipi anavassutā hoti;
root_text.an3.109:5.3: kūṭampi apūtikaṁ hoti, gopānasiyopi apūtikā honti, bhittipi apūtikā hoti.
root_text.an3.109:6.1: Evamevaṁ kho, gahapati, citte rakkhite kāyakammampi rakkhitaṁ hoti, vacīkammampi rakkhitaṁ hoti, manokammampi rakkhitaṁ hoti.
root_text.an3.109:6.2: Tassa rakkhitakāyakammantassa rakkhitavacīkammantassa rakkhitamanokammantassa kāyakammampi anavassutaṁ hoti, vacīkammampi anavassutaṁ hoti, manokammampi anavassutaṁ hoti.
root_text.an3.109:6.3: Tassa anavassutakāyakammantassa anavassutavacīkammantassa anavassutamanokammantassa kāyakammampi apūtikaṁ hoti, vacīkammampi apūtikaṁ hoti, manokammampi apūtikaṁ hoti.
root_text.an3.109:6.4: Tassa apūtikāyakammantassa apūtivacīkammantassa apūtimanokammantassa bhaddakaṁ maraṇaṁ hoti, bhaddikā kālakiriyā”ti.

translation_text.an3.109:1.1: Then the householder Anāthapiṇḍika went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him:
translation_text.an3.109:1.2: “Householder, when the mind is unprotected, deeds of body, speech, and mind are unprotected.
translation_text.an3.109:1.3: When deeds are unprotected, they fester.
translation_text.an3.109:1.4: When deeds fester, they become rotten.
translation_text.an3.109:1.5: Someone whose deeds of body, speech, and mind are rotten will not have a good death.
translation_text.an3.109:2.1: It’s like a bungalow with a bad roof. The roof peak, rafters, and walls are unprotected.
translation_text.an3.109:3.1: In the same way, when the mind is unprotected, bodily, verbal, and mental deeds are unprotected. …
translation_text.an3.109:3.4: Someone whose deeds of body, speech, and mind are rotten will not have a good death.
translation_text.an3.109:4.1: When the mind is protected, bodily, verbal, and mental deeds are protected.
translation_text.an3.109:4.2: When deeds are protected, they don’t fester.
translation_text.an3.109:4.3: When deeds don’t fester, they don’t become rotten.
translation_text.an3.109:4.4: Someone whose deeds of body, speech, and mind aren’t rotten will have a good death.
translation_text.an3.109:5.1: It’s like a bungalow with a good roof. The roof peak, rafters, and walls are protected.
translation_text.an3.109:6.1: In the same way, when the mind is protected, bodily, verbal, and mental deeds are protected. …
translation_text.an3.109:6.4: Someone whose deeds of body, speech, and mind aren’t rotten will have a good death.”
